skill.name: protocol-standardization
skill.description: Standardize fragmented experimental steps into reproducible protocol documents when you need method organization, lab SOP drafting, or cross-operator reproducibility; missing parameters must be explicitly marked as "To be supplemented/Not provided".
skill.summary: Converts messy experimental notes into structured, reproducible protocol documents with explicit parameter tracking and completeness enforcement.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: All 13 HIGH severity 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' findings are FALSE POSITIVES - no cryptographic algorithms exist in this documentation-only skill. All 7 MEDIUM 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' findings are FALSE POSITIVES - Markdown code formatting backticks were misidentified as shell execution. All 3 LOW 'System reconnaissance' findings are FALSE POSITIVES - text refers to skill boundary constraints, not system scanning. One TRUE POSITIVE: hardcoded GitHub URL in SKILL.md line 7 (legitimate source reference, low risk). No executable code, scripts, or binaries present. No prompt injection detected. Overall risk level: LOW.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded GitHub Source URL
low_findings.description: SKILL.md contains a hardcoded URL linking to the skill's GitHub source repository. This is a legitimate reference URL for attribution and provenance tracking, not a data exfiltration endpoint. Low risk.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URL points to the skill's own source repository on GitHub. This is standard attribution practice for open-source skills and poses no security risk.

content.user_title: Standardize Experimental Protocols for Reproducible Research
content.value_statement: Fragmented experimental notes often lead to irreproducible results across lab teams. This skill converts messy notes, chat logs, and bullet points into formal, standardized protocol documents with explicit parameter tracking and completeness checking.
actual_capabilities: Converts fragmented experimental notes into structured protocol documents with preparation, execution, and closing phases
Enforces parameter completeness by flagging missing values as To be supplemented/Not provided
Standardizes units, terminology, and step formatting across protocol documents
Adds operational checks including logical ordering, safety notes, and waste disposal requirements
Supports reusable protocol templates for consistent output formatting
limitations: Cannot validate the scientific accuracy of experimental steps or parameters
Does not generate missing parameter values; users must supply them separately
Does not execute or automate any laboratory procedures
Standardization quality depends on completeness of the input notes provided

use_cases.title: Lab SOP Standardization
use_cases.description: Convert fragmented lab notes from multiple operators into a single standardized protocol document with consistent formatting and parameter tracking.

use_cases.title: Method Publication Support
use_cases.description: Transform exploratory experimental notes into structured protocols suitable for method sections in papers or supplementary materials.

use_cases.title: Team Onboarding Documentation
use_cases.description: Create clear, reproducible protocols for new team members to follow when learning lab procedures and techniques.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Protocol from Messy Notes
prompt_templates.prompt: I have the following experimental notes about [experiment type]. Please convert them into a standardized protocol document using prep/execution/closing structure. Mark any missing parameters as 'To be supplemented/Not provided': [paste notes here]
prompt_templates.scenario: A researcher has rough bullet-point notes and needs a formal protocol

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Operator Protocol Standardization
prompt_templates.prompt: I have [number] different versions of the same [experiment type] protocol recorded by different team members. Please merge them into a single standardized protocol, highlighting any conflicting parameters that need resolution: [paste protocols here]
prompt_templates.scenario: A lab manager needs to unify protocols from different team members

prompt_templates.title: Protocol Audit for Missing Parameters
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this existing protocol and identify all critical parameters that are missing or underspecified. Categorize gaps by risk level and provide a checklist of parameters that must be supplemented before the protocol can be considered reproducible: [paste protocol here]
prompt_templates.scenario: A quality assurance officer reviewing existing protocols for completeness

prompt_templates.title: Cross-Site SOP with Safety and QC
prompt_templates.prompt: Standardize this experimental protocol for use across [number] research sites. Include site-specific safety notes, waste disposal procedures, and quality control checkpoints. Each site may have different equipment, so mark equipment-dependent parameters clearly: [paste protocol here]
prompt_templates.scenario: A principal investigator standardizing a protocol across multiple research sites

output_examples.input: Raw notes: Add acetone to lysate, mix. Put at cold temperature for a while. Spin down, remove supernatant. Dry pellet then resuspend.
output: Standardized Protocol: Protein Precipitation by Cold Acetone
Preparation:
1. Pre-chill acetone to ___ (To be supplemented/Not provided)
2. Label microcentrifuge tubes with sample IDs
Execution:
3. Add pre-chilled acetone to lysate at ratio ___ (To be supplemented/Not provided)
4. Incubate at ___ for ___ minutes (To be supplemented/Not provided)
5. Centrifuge at ___ xg for ___ minutes at ___ C (To be supplemented/Not provided)
6. Carefully remove and discard supernatant without disturbing pellet
Closing:
7. Air-dry pellet for ___ minutes (To be supplemented/Not provided)
8. Resuspend pellet in ___ buffer volume ___ uL (To be supplemented/Not provided)
Safety: Acetone handling PPE requirements (To be supplemented/Not provided)
Waste: Solvent waste disposal route (To be supplemented/Not provided)

output_examples.input: PCR setup notes: add template DNA, primers, master mix. Run in thermocycler. Check on gel.
output: Standardized Protocol: PCR Amplification
Preparation:
1. Prepare reaction mix: template ___ ng, primers ___ uM each, master mix ___ uL
2. Add nuclease-free water to ___ uL total volume (To be supplemented/Not provided)
3. Mix gently by pipetting and spin down briefly
Execution:
4. Place in thermocycler with program: ___ (To be supplemented/Not provided)
5. Initial denaturation: ___ C for ___ minutes (To be supplemented/Not provided)
6. 35 cycles of: denature ___ C ___ sec, anneal ___ C ___ sec, extend ___ C ___ sec
7. Final extension: ___ C for ___ minutes (To be supplemented/Not provided)
8. Hold at 4 C until analysis
Closing:
9. Analyze by gel electrophoresis on ___ percent agarose at ___ V for ___ minutes
QC: Expected band size ___ bp (To be supplemented/Not provided)

best_practices: Always include the raw input notes alongside the standardized output to maintain an auditable transformation record
Review all flagged To be supplemented parameters as a checklist and fill them in before using the protocol in the lab
Use the same template structure consistently across all protocols in your lab to enable cross-comparison between experiments
anti_patterns: Do not fabricate or infer missing parameter values; always mark unknowns as To be supplemented/Not provided
Do not skip safety and waste disposal sections even for routine or familiar procedures
Do not mix unit systems (metric and imperial) within the same protocol document

faq.question: Can this skill generate experimental data?
faq.answer: No. The skill only reorganizes and standardizes existing information. It cannot create or validate scientific data.

faq.question: What happens if I do not know a parameter value?
faq.answer: Unknown values are marked as To be supplemented/Not provided. The skill never fabricates missing information.

faq.question: Can this skill write a complete research paper?
faq.answer: No. The skill is limited to protocol and method documentation. It does not write full research papers or abstracts.

faq.question: Does this skill work with clinical protocols?
faq.answer: The skill stays on the planning and documentation side. It does not provide clinical or diagnostic advice.

faq.question: What input formats does this skill accept?
faq.answer: Any text-based notes including chat logs, bullet points, notebook fragments, and existing protocol drafts.

faq.question: Can I customize the output template structure?
faq.answer: Yes. You can modify the template in the assets/protocol_template.md file to match your lab preferred format.
